prunes_patterns: q8 activation produced in a separate pack kernel
dot4-only decode win
entries.reason: The activation producer cost erases the native dot4/MMVQ consumer gain at reuse_count=2.

prunes_patterns: single bounded AMD DSL tweak for q8 decode
COMGR source reshuffle
entries.reason: The passing artifact route is concrete, but tinygrad-owned COMGR/ASM variants miss the schedule and no bounded A2 feature clears the gate.

prunes_patterns: single q4k verify kernel
spec decode as one-kernel shortcut
entries.reason: Verify T-scaling is distributed across attention, Q4_K, Q6_K, and fast-path loss.

prunes_patterns: current T>1 verify path
spec decode without T-cheap target verify
entries.reason: The current T=5 verify costs 4.66x one T=1 pass, so spec only reopens if the verify lifecycle changes.

prunes_patterns: bounded WMMA knob sweep
LDS-only prefill matmul row
entries.reason: Pure tinygrad bounded WMMA/LDS sweeps stay near the ~42 TFLOPS plateau; Tensile-class transfer is project-level.

prunes_patterns: in-process rocBLAS on tinygrad pointers
HIP runtime bridge lane A
entries.reason: HIP runtime and tinygrad HCQ/KFD are mutually exclusive in-process; extracted HSACO through HCQ is the viable artifact boundary.

prunes_patterns: reuse-free flash-prefill
global-reread attention rewrite
entries.reason: Correct attention math without K/V locality is not a performance primitive.

prunes_patterns: fuse silu(gate)*up into producer/down GEMV to remove E_49152 launch
decode kernel-count reduction as a speedup lever
entries.reason: Folding silu(gate)*up into the up GEMV is byte-exact but 0% faster: the activation work is CONSERVED (runs serially after the reduce, same ~33us) and the launch itself is ~free (decode is GPU-exec-bound, D~=W). llama gets it free by hiding the ALU under HBM-bound mmvq latency; naive fusion cannot. Recovery needs activation/HBM-latency overlap inside the GEMV inner loop, not fusion.

prunes_patterns: fuse flash reduce/fixup rows to remove cross-chunk overhead
treat reduce_fixup as fusible fixup
entries.reason: The attention 'reduce_fixup' bucket is dominated by the Q@KT SCORE matmul (r_*) and its ctx-slope is the O(KV) symbolic-length score reduce; softmax_stats' slope is flash_prob (O(KV) exp/key). These are intrinsic O(KV) compute, not fusible overhead. Only ~0.5ms of FIXED helper kernels are fusible, and those are work-conserved (see ffn_activation_fusion_work_conserved).

prunes_patterns: fully-fused Q@KT+softmax+P@V single flash kernel
coupled multi-accumulator reduce in one decode kernel
entries.reason: tinygrad's flash-decode is split into 6 kernels BECAUSE coupled multi-accumulator reduces trip the linearizer's range-ordering (qk_flash_decode.py:73-80). The fully-fused flash kernel is the same codegen wall as the prefill POWN/software-pipelined-K-loop (BEAM-hang class). Multi-week linearizer project, not a bounded fusion build.

prunes_patterns: raise FLASH_L to reduce chunk count
entries.reason: FLASH_L=256/512 regress: partial_compute parallelism collapses (fewer batch-1 workgroups) faster than reduce/stat shrinks. ctx4096 53.9 vs 60.8 tok/s. L=128 default is optimal.

prunes_patterns: bypass flash with SDPA at short context
entries.reason: FLASH_DECODE=0 catastrophic: batch-1 SDPA ~1% GPU occupancy. 10.4 vs 66.5 tok/s @1024. Threshold-512 cutover already correct.

prunes_patterns: remove the FFN silu .contiguous() to drop E_49152
entries.reason: Removing .contiguous() leaves E_49152 unchanged (1.22 vs 1.24ms): the custom ffn_down GEMV forces the activation to materialize regardless.

prunes_patterns: fewer-kernel fused flash tile as a bounded win
fusion/launch-reduction alone for attention
entries.reason: The raw C fully-fused flash tile (2 kernels: qk+softmax+V interleaved + split-reduce) is byte-EXACT but 2.5-3.3x SLOWER than the optimized-but-split gqa_coop_vec 6-kernel path (266 vs 106us @ctx1024, 553 vs 165us @ctx4096, warm-JIT fair). The naive fused kernel lacks GQA V-reuse + coalescing; those per-kernel losses dwarf the fusion benefit. A winning tile needs fusion AND coop opts in one kernel (llama flash_attn_tile) = linearizer-walled / raw-bridge codegen, not a bounded patch. (Earlier 41x 'win' was a UOp-not-JITted artifact.)

prunes_patterns: fuse silu(gate)*up into the ffn_down GEMV input prologue for latency hiding
entries.reason: The q6k coop ffn_down GEMV reads each activation element once PER OUTPUT ROW (4096x); fusing silu*up inline recomputes the activation 4096x = blowup. The kernel only shares loads within a workgroup's row_tile, not across all rows, so there is no load-once-reuse-across-all-rows to hang activation latency-hiding on. Combined with Phase B1 work-conservation, the FFN activation latency-hiding lane is closed.

prunes_patterns: fused single-kernel scalar flash tile for DECODE
port lds_attention_tile (prefill) to decode for a speedup
add LDS+GQA to the raw fused tile to beat gqa_coop_vec
entries.reason: MEASURED: fused tile w/ LDS K/V reuse + GQA is byte-exact but 0.21x@1024 / 0.12x@4096 vs gqa_coop_vec -- WORSE than raw fused (0.40x). Classified: (1) OCCUPANCY: GQA consolidation drops workgroups 4x (per-kv-head vs per-query-head)+serial G loop, coop reuse REMOVED the GPU-filling parallelism; (2) q.k COMPUTE-MAPPING: 128 d-threads each recompute the full dot (ALU redundancy LDS can't fix), gqa_coop_vec does q.k ONCE via matmul; (3) REGIME MISMATCH (root): lds_attention_tile wins at PREFILL via T multi-query parallelism, DECODE has T=1 so no query-parallelism to fill the workgroup. NOT LDS/barrier, NOT V-reuse (exact). Only remaining path = WMMA/tensor-core flash tile (deep RDNA3-WMMA codegen, multi-week). Fused-scalar-tile lever REFUTED at decode shape.

prunes_patterns: WMMA/tensor-core flash decode kernel
assume llama decode attention uses matrix cores
entries.reason: AUDITED (rocprofv3 + llama source): llama DECODE attention is flash_attn_tile, a PURE VECTOR ggml_cuda_mad body. rocWMMA fattn is OFF by default (CMakeLists.txt:219) AND T=1 is below the tensor-core threshold (Q.ne1*gqa_ratio=4 > 8 is false, fattn.cu:519). The WMMA flash_attn_ext_f16 runs ONLY in prefill (n=72). So there is NO tensor-core attention trick to replicate; the decode win is parallel-block decomposition + LDS, not matrix hardware.

prunes_patterns: reopen Q4_K/Q6_K MMVQ for a decode wall-time win
port llama int8 dp4a + q8 reuse for speed
entries.reason: AUDITED: llama mmvq 7.69ms/tok vs tinygrad weight-GEMV 7.93ms @ctx1024 = +0.24ms PARITY. llama's int8 sudot4(V_DOT4) + q8_1 reuse + gate/up fusion is a STRUCTURAL body diff but NOT a wall-time gap -- both HBM-bandwidth-bound on the weight read at ~similar GB/s. mmvq is 77-81% of llama decode but at parity with tinygrad. The gap is attention, not mmvq.

prunes_patterns: bounded non-WMMA vector flash-decode tile (split-count + warp-coop dot) to beat gqa_coop_vec
naive per-key hand tile for decode attention
entries.reason: MEASURED 2026-06-21: many KV-splits (0.21->0.51x, fixes occupancy) + warp-cooperative ds_bpermute q.k (->0.60x, byte-exact) IMPROVE but do NOT clear >=1.05x vs gqa_coop_vec @ctx1024 (best 0.60x; 0.96x@4096). Time flat across split count = WORK-bound on the per-key serial warp-reduce loop. gqa_coop_vec's q.k is a MATMUL (efficient GEMM) that beats a hand-rolled per-key warp loop; llama's flash_attn_tile (9.2us, 11x faster than gqa_coop_vec) needs nbatch_fa=64 K-tile batching + vectorized fp16 + register blocking = multi-day faithful port (north-star codegen), NOT a bounded build. Lever is real (llama proves it) but only the full-engineering port clears the gate -> REST_DECODE.

prunes_patterns: FLASH_L downward (more splits) on gqa_coop_vec as a whole-decode win
split-count tuning to promote decode
entries.reason: MEASURED 2026-06-21 (principle-aligned: more splits on the EXISTING winner, preserves matmul+GQA reuse, no workgroup reduction): FLASH_L=64 (64->128 workgroups) = 1.08x standalone attention @ctx1024, byte-exact -- PASSES the >=1.05x first gate (principle CONFIRMED). BUT W==D whole-decode = +1.8%@1024 / +2.8%@512 / -1.2%@4096 -- MISSES the >=5% promotion gate and REGRESSES at 4096. Two ceilings: attention is only ~23% of decode (1.08x -> ~1.8% whole), and tinygrad's combine-over-splits caps useful splits at ~16-32 (finer regresses) vs llama's 48-144 -- needs stream-k/efficient many-split combine = deep engineering. Sweet spot L=64; L<=16 regresses. REST_DECODE; FLASH_L=64 a marginal byte-exact ~2% short-ctx opt-in for owner only.

prunes_patterns: warp-cooperative fused q.k flash tile as a local win vs gqa_coop_vec
improving the split-combine (more workgroups / stream-k) as the lever to beat gqa_coop_vec
entries.reason: MEASURED 2026-06-21 (first real north-star candidate executed through the binding system; byte-exact err 0.0, clock-pinned): the warp-cooperative flash_attn_tile (ds_bpermute q.k, LDS K/V, GQA pack, register online softmax, 128-1024 partial workgroups) scores 0.58x@ctx1024 / 0.89x@ctx4096 vs gqa_coop_vec -> FAIL_LOCAL_AB. ATTRIBUTION refutes the prior 'serial-combine-is-the-ceiling' hypothesis: (a) the warp PARTIAL ALONE (~111us@1024) already EXCEEDS coop's whole attention (~109us) -> even a free combine cannot win; (b) a many-workgroup combine (streamk, 128 wg) is byte-for-byte as slow as the serial flash_reduce (32 wg) -> the combine is HBM-bandwidth-bound, NOT launch/occupancy-bound. The real ceiling is the FUSED cooperative q.k partial vs coop's SEPARATED optimized matmul (tinygrad GEMM for scores) + weighted-sum partial + optimized multi-kernel combine. A future candidate must produce a faster q.k partial, not a better combine. gqa_coop_vec stands; bounded standalone tiles remain refuted.

blocked_by_refutations: separate q8 pack cost erases dot4 gain
native tinygrad ASM/COMGR variants fail perf
no bounded AMD DSL feature closes artifact gap
verify cost distributed across attention + Q4_K + Q6_K
current T>1 verify path does not amortize weights
SCR-0..4 found no bounded attention or grouped-linears component route
in-process HIP runtime bridge killed; HCQ artifact route required
bounded WMMA/LDS knob sweeps plateau near 42 TFLOPS
naive multi-wave LDS occupancy/barrier tax; not yet double-buffered
reuse-free attention is not a performance primitive
